intro: × Sorbaronia arsenii, also known as hybrid whitebeam, is a rare, sterile hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Sorbus aucuparia (European whitebeam) and S. torminalis (wild service tree). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, colds, and flu. The bioactive constituents of × Sorbaronia arsenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant's bark and leaves include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
